name: Lorehold
focus: History, archaeology, spirits, and the magic of the past
philosophy: The study of history is itself an act of magic. To know the past is to understand the present — and to command the future.
aesthetic: Ancient ruins, spirit echoes, torch-lit excavations, crumbling tablets, and the weight of ages
campus_vibe: Like wandering through a museum that occasionally talks back. Crumbling architecture meets active excavation. Students debate over fossils and manifested spirits drift through lecture halls.
mascot_desc: Manifested spirits of scholars past sometimes serve as mentors and assistants on campus.
student_stereotypes: History obsessive who references ancient civilizations at every opportunity
Field archaeologist type who'd rather dig than study
Spirit-talker who holds conversations with hundred-year-old echoes
The one who's definitely going to accidentally wake something up
magic_focus: Order and chaos magic, spirit binding, temporal echoes, ruins-walking
summary: Lorehold scholars believe that history is a living force. They commune with spirit echoes of the past, excavate ancient ruins, and wield the magic of order and chaos to understand how civilizations rise and fall. Lorehold students tend to be passionate, curious, and occasionally reckless in pursuit of knowledge.

name: Prismari
tagline: Art Is the Lens Through Which We See the World
focus: Elemental magic channeled through art, performance, and creative expression
philosophy: Magic without artistry is just noise. True mastery is expressing the raw power of the elements through something beautiful, something felt.
aesthetic: Vibrant murals that move, fire sculptures, cascading water installations, living paintings, performances that summon storms
campus_vibe: An explosion of color and sound. Every corridor is a gallery, every courtyard a stage. Students rehearse elemental displays between classes and critique each other's creative choices constantly.
mascot_desc: Elemental art constructs roam campus as both decoration and guardians.
student_stereotypes: The drama student who treats every conversation as a performance
The visionary artist who sees connections no one else does
The passionate creator who burns (sometimes literally) with intensity
The one who turns every assignment into a statement piece
magic_focus: Elemental manipulation, artistic conjuration, performance magic, emotional resonance casting
summary: Prismari students channel elemental magic through pure artistic expression. Their spells are performances, their performances are spells. Whether composing fire symphonies or sculpting water into living portraits, Prismari students believe magic and art are inseparable. They tend toward big personalities and even bigger creative visions.

name: Quandrix
focus: Mathematics, geometry, patterns in nature, fractal magic, and theoretical arcane studies
philosophy: Reality is a pattern. Magic is the language of that pattern. The universe is a theorem waiting to be proven.
aesthetic: Impossible geometries, fractal gardens, hedge mazes that rearrange themselves, equations floating in mid-air
campus_vibe: Quiet, cerebral, occasionally disorienting. Students sit under impossible topiaries discussing whether free will is an emergent property of mathematical systems. The architecture does not obey normal spatial rules.
mascot_desc: Living fractal constructs serve as tutors, assistants, and reminders that some shapes never truly end.
student_stereotypes: The brilliant theorist who finds everything more interesting as a math problem
The nature pattern-watcher who sees fractals everywhere
The abstract thinker who sometimes forgets to eat while solving equations
The one who questions the premise of every question
magic_focus: Mathematics-based spellcasting, spatial manipulation, fractal duplication, nature pattern magic
summary: Quandrix mages see magic as an expression of mathematical truth. They study the patterns underlying nature itself — fractals, sequences, and geometries — and bend reality by understanding its underlying code. Quandrix students are typically brilliant, philosophical, and prone to conversations that spiral into fascinating abstraction.

name: Silverquill
focus: Language, rhetoric, debate, light and shadow magic, and the power of the written and spoken word
philosophy: Words are the sharpest weapons and the softest comfort. To master language is to master minds, hearts, and the battlefield.
aesthetic: Elegant black and silver. Ink that moves. Poetry that manifests as light or shadow. Debate halls where words leave visible marks in the air.
campus_vibe: Sophisticated and slightly cutthroat. Students debate over everything — not for sport, but because they believe ideas matter. Campus feels like a mix of literary salon and dueling ground, all done in impeccable style.
mascot_desc: Ink constructs that serve as writing assistants, messengers, and reminders that every word has power.
student_stereotypes: The eloquent debater who never loses an argument and knows it
The poet-mage whose words literally cut
The stylish intellectual who looks effortlessly perfect at all times
The one who will destroy you in an argument and then offer you tea
magic_focus: Radiance and shadow magic, linguistic enchantment, written word conjuration, debate-based casting
summary: Silverquill students wield language as magic and magic as language. Through radiance and shadow, they give power to words — inspiring allies, terrifying enemies, or simply winning every argument. They tend to be polished, sharp-tongued, and very aware of how they're perceived. Also the most stylish college on campus.

name: Witherbloom
focus: Life, death, decay, growth, herbalism, and the biological magic of living and dying
philosophy: Nothing truly dies. Every ending is a beginning. The boundary between life and death is not a wall but a door, and we hold the key.
aesthetic: Dense swampy grounds, bioluminescent fungi, skeletal herbarium specimens, blooming flowers growing from decay, cauldrons of life essence
campus_vibe: Earthy, slightly eerie, deeply practical. Students wade through bog gardens comparing decomposition rates. The campus smells of rain and rich earth and sometimes something harder to name. Deeply kind people, deeply comfortable with mortality.
mascot_desc: Magical pests and critters are treated as fellow students of nature on Witherbloom campus.
student_stereotypes: The herbalist who carries seventeen different potion components at all times
The one who thinks skeletons are just a different kind of alive
The pragmatist who sees life and death as tools, not judgments
The swamp witch aesthetic devotee who is also somehow a gifted healer
magic_focus: Life and death magic, herbalism, biological manipulation, essence draining and restoration, healing
summary: Witherbloom mages study the boundary between life and death, treating both as sources of magical power. They brew potions, commune with living ecosystems, and wield the energy of growing and dying things. Witherbloom students are practical, empathetic, and deeply comfortable with the parts of nature others find unsettling.
